source.title: Vitamin D - Health Professional Fact Sheet

types.name: Vitamin D2 (ergocalciferol)
types.source: Manufactured from yeast using UV irradiation

types.name: Vitamin D3 (cholecalciferol)
types.note: D3 increases and maintains serum 25(OH)D levels more effectively than D2

deficiency_effects.children: Rickets: soft bones, skeletal deformities, developmental delays, seizures, cardiac complications
deficiency_effects.adults: Osteomalacia: weak bones, bone pain, deformities, dental abnormalities
high_risk_groups: Older adults (declining skin synthesis with age)

bone_health.finding: Small increases in bone mineral density with vitamin D + calcium supplementation in postmenopausal women and older men
bone_health.VITAL_2022: 50 mcg (2,000 IU) daily vitamin D3 did not reduce fracture risk in generally healthy older adults (n=25,871)

cancer.finding: Does not affect cancer incidence but reduces total cancer mortality by 12-13% (meta-analysis of clinical trials)
cancer.VITAL_trial: No significant differences in breast, prostate, or colorectal cancer rates

cardiovascular.finding: Clinical trials provide little support for cardiovascular protective effects
cardiovascular.trials: VITAL trial and New Zealand trial found no protective effects on heart attacks, strokes, or cardiovascular mortality

depression.finding: Meta-analysis of 9 trials: no significant symptom reduction with supplementation
depression.note: Vitamin D receptors exist in brain areas linked to depression

type2_diabetes.trial: Major trial (n=2,423 prediabetic): vitamin D did not prevent diabetes progression in those with adequate baseline levels

multiple_sclerosis.observational: Adequate vitamin D might reduce risk and slow progression
multiple_sclerosis.cochrane_review: 12 clinical trials: supplementation had no effect on relevant clinical outcomes including relapse

health_research.immune_function: Modulates inflammatory pathways; specific clinical benefits remain under investigation

drug_interactions.effect: May reduce endogenous synthesis; vitamin D may reduce statin efficacy
Impair vitamin D metabolism; deficiency rates doubled in users

toxicity.severe_cases: Renal failure, soft tissue calcification, cardiac arrhythmias, death
toxicity.note: Toxicity stems almost exclusively from supplementation, never from sun exposure
